title: cattleya × dolosa
intro: Cattleya × dolosa, also known as the 'Dolosa Cattleya' or 'Paphiopedilum × dolosa', is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Paphiopedilum victoria-regina and Paphiopedilum victoria-regina var. victoria-regina, which is a member of the Paphiopedilum genus. The medicinal properties of Cattleya × dolosa include its potential use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant are said to include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Cattleya × dolosa is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are believed to offer therapeutic benefits when consumed or applied topically.
